column1: # ONE table, run by every language mirror so they cannot drift:
# Tab-separated.  Column 1 is the case kind; remaining columns depend on kind.
# Empty fields are written as empty strings between tabs (trailing tabs matter).
# malformed suffixes stay opaque - the whole token is the family
# -- stderiv domain: family test, NOT a "stderiv" prefix test -----------------
# -- the new tokens parse through the existing naming paths -------------------
# ── resolve_any: the ONE physical copy of a Shared artifact (res, spk) ───────
# resolve() walks a fixed method -> standard -> untagged list and so misses a
# copy written under any other token, including every suffixed one -- which no
# fixed list can enumerate.  resolve_any scans instead.  Same columns as resolve:
# preferred absent -> fall through the standard/stderiv/sdiff order
# THE POINT: a suffixed token no fixed list names is still found by scanning
# a known token still beats an arbitrary one
# untagged legacy name is the last resort, after every tagged copy
# a longer stage tag is NOT a method token and must not be resolved to
# nothing present -> not found, path points at where the preferred copy would be
# group is part of the match: a copy for another group must not be returned
